findings.title: FUSE in this session was stale on operator-directives at run start; PROP-066's Step 4c only syncs the session it runs in
findings.evidence: Pre-flight directive scan from FUSE returned 7 'pending' directives (003, 005, 006, 007, plus older 002/028/025-003 still labeled 'open' which the dispatcher filters out). Cross-check against git clone showed DIRECTIVE-004 and DIRECTIVE-005 were 'completed' in git but 'pending' in FUSE. Root cause: operator-directives/ is classified 'append_only' in build.js OWNERSHIP, so walkAppendOnly skips existing files (additive-edit exception allowed by CLAUDE.md DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE rule transfers ONLY via the agent's authoritative clone — sync-workspace doesn't overwrite append_only files in FUSE). When workspace-sync runs in session A (e.g., affectionate-intelligent-mayer at 09:07Z), it only updates session A's FUSE. Session B (lucid-affectionate-mccarthy, mine) sees stale FUSE indefinitely.
findings.impact: Tinker's pre-flight directive scan reads from cwd-relative path; cwd starts as the FUSE workspace, so scans get stale state. Mitigation in this run: cross-check against git clone, take the git state as authoritative. Same risk applies to any agent that reads operator-directives from FUSE — currently only tinker.

options.name: Tinker pre-flight always clones first, reads from clone
Sync extend walkAppendOnly to overwrite operator-directives files (status-transition specific)

findings.recommendation: option (a) — minimal prompt-edit. Add to tinker.md dispatcher pre-flight: 'Before reading operator-directives, ensure you are reading from the most recent git state. If you are running from FUSE, either fresh-clone first or  to read the git-authoritative version.' File as a separate PROP later if recurrence justifies it.
